questions.text: The value of acceleration due to gravity on Earth is approximately:
questions.explanation: Acceleration due to gravity (g) on Earth's surface is approximately 9.8 m/s².

questions.text: Mass is the same everywhere in the universe.
questions.explanation: Mass is the amount of matter in an object and remains constant. Weight changes with gravity, but mass does not.

questions.text: State the universal law of gravitation.
questions.correctAnswer: Every object attracts every other object with force proportional to product of masses and inversely proportional to square of distance.
questions.explanation: F = G(m₁m₂)/r², where G is universal gravitational constant (6.67 × 10⁻¹¹ Nm²/kg²).

questions.text: The force of attraction between Earth and an object is called ________.
questions.explanation: The force with which Earth attracts an object is called gravity. The weight of an object is the force of gravity acting on it.
